title: Recurring notifications (reminders) after Timeline Due Date passes
excerpt: Recurring reminders for overdue items only work with Date columns but not with Timeline columns. This should be fixed by monday.com as timeline columns have the same data as Date columns but more so should be used more i…

posts.author: leonidas (Leonidas)
posts.cookedHtml: Recurring reminders for overdue items only work with Date columns but not with Timeline columns. This should be fixed by monday.com as timeline columns have the same data as Date columns but more so should be used more instead of the focus of developers being on the Date column as it has been since inception. I cannot find a workaround to set a new Due Date column to be udpated accordingly to the Timeline End Date Column, only by using the formula column which they cannot be used to set recurring notifications for overdue items. Seems like a simple thing to solve but I can’t find a solution. Any ideas : ) ?

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:

posts.author: leonidas (Leonidas)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately our company policy is that we do not use integrations due to cybersecurity reasons (data privacy) and technical reasons (bugs etc.), which is why we ended up choosing monday.com as it is already has pretty comprehensive solutions embedded and constantly improves. However thank you for the prompt assistance!
